title: WhoAmI
description: When running on servers with "online_mode = false", this mod attempts to find a player's real UUID instead of assigning a random offline UUID.
body:
WhoAmI

Resolves UUID issues on offline mode Servers.

## How this works: - When joining a server that is in Offline Mode (aka: `online-mode=false`), the mod checks if your username actually exists. If it does, you won't be assigned a random UUID and will instead receive your real UUID. - This mod is also server-sided, so players do not need to install it. ## Why this useful: - If you ever switched a server from `online-mode=true` to `online-mode=false`, you probably had to deal with data loss. This mod resolves this issue. - Also if you ever tried using cosmetic mods on `online-mode=false` servers you also probably noticed that your cosmetics are gone. (For Example if you're using [Arsenal](https://modrinth.com/mod/arsenal), and you have the supporter bonuses, they don't work on `online-mode=false` servers. This Mod Fixes that issue too.) - I've only tested this with [Arsenal](https://modrinth.com/mod/arsenal) and The [Essential Mod](https://modrinth.com/mod/essential), and they both work! - Also you can use this mod with [ForceTablistHeads](https://modrinth.com/mod/forcetablistheads) if you want. ## Here's an Example of using WhoAmI with Arsenal: ![Example](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/1ba84236803dad58cfcfea52934ec2bfbd4507bb.png) ## QnA: Q: Can I port this mod to another version or mod loader? A: Sure, go ahead, but credit me somewhere. Q: Will WhoAmI make `mod x` work? A: I've already listed the mods that were tested with it. Q: Is this dangerous for my server? A: No. This mod simply checks Mojang's API for your UUID and assigns it to you if you have a Minecraft account.
